target.profile_basis: rch Criterion cache_baseline ranked cache-key construction as the hottest fj-cache surface in this pass.

baseline_profile.command: RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/frankenjax-fj-cache-profile rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-cache --bench cache_baseline -- --sample-size 10 --measurement-time 1 --warm-up-time 1

rejected_candidate.reason: Criterion rebench worsened cache_key/streaming/10eqn_program on worker vmi1156319 from the prior worker baseline shape to 774.07 ns mean, so the candidate was reverted and not shipped.

opportunity_matrix.hotspot: write_len_prefixed -> write_usize_decimal during canonical cache-key payload streaming

one_lever.description: Fast-path one- and two-digit usize decimal length frames before falling back to the existing stack-buffer reverse digit loop.
one_lever.scope: Only decimal formatting for length prefixes changed; payload field order, labels, delimiters, SHA-256 input bytes, and digest hex formatting remain otherwise unchanged.

isomorphism_proof.ordering_preserved: yes: write_len_prefixed still writes decimal length, colon, payload bytes, semicolon in the same order for every field.
isomorphism_proof.tie_breaking_unchanged: N/A: cache-key payload construction has no tie-breaking.
isomorphism_proof.floating_point: N/A: no floating-point operations are introduced or reordered.
isomorphism_proof.rng: N/A: no RNG state is read or written.
isomorphism_proof.errors: unchanged: write_usize_decimal is infallible before and after.

validation: rch exec -- cargo clippy -p fj-cache --all-targets -- -D warnings
notes: A subagent pass for this target hit a usage limit; the optimization loop continued serially with explicit rch baseline, rebench, and proof artifacts.
The dirty worktree also contains separate fj-cache in-memory key-storage work for a different bead; this evidence and commit intentionally isolate only decimal length framing.
